channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: The Internet Is No Longer Human
item.description: AI Revolution: The video argues that AI bots now constitute the majority of web traffic, with AI systems answering queries before users click links, and AI agents autonomously browsing and purchasing online. It positions this shift as a fundamental change in internet usage patterns, where machines are increasingly intermediating human-web interaction.

item.title: STOP Using Claude Code Without This Fable 5 Agentic OS
item.description: Chase AI: Fable 5 Agentic OS is a system architecture for running Claude Code with modular capabilities across memory, observability, and skill management. The video presents it as a production-ready framework for building autonomous agents using Claude Code as the underlying model.

item.title: Stop Prop Drilling Your React Modals (Use react-call)
item.description: Better Stack: react-call is a lightweight utility library that allows React components to be invoked as asynchronous functions. It eliminates prop drilling and state management complexity in modals through three architectural patterns: Root and Stack for layout isolation, Upsert for singleton management, and Mutation Flow for handling backend loading states.

item.title: Type This Into Claude, It'll Make You Build 10x Faster.
item.description: Austin Marchese: Austin Marchese presents six power phrases for prompting Claude Code that reportedly accelerate development cycles by 10x. The techniques include strategies used by Claude Code's creator and Andrej Karpathy, plus guidance on a commonly misused phrase that costs developers time and money.

item.title: How to ACTUALLY Use AI For Coding
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts demonstrates a /goal command for OpenAI's Codex that enables autonomous code generation via a loop called Ralph. The tool accepts high-level English descriptions of desired outcomes and handles planning, execution, and self-correction without requiring user intervention at each step.

item.title: Bridging Design Systems and Code with MCP and AI Agents
item.description: IBM Technology: Design systems and context engineering are presented as techniques to guide AI agents in building consistent and correct applications. MCP (Model Context Protocol) is positioned as a mechanism to connect design systems with intelligent agents for coordinated software development.

item.title: Build a Hermes Knowledge Base That Self-Improves
item.description: Jack Roberts: A workflow combining Obsidian, Claude, NotebookLM, and Granola to create a self-improving knowledge base for AI agents like Hermes. The system ingests meeting notes, emails, and research through automated cron jobs and indexing, enabling persistent memory across sessions using LLM wiki principles.

item.title: OpenRouter Fusion: They OFFICIALLY CLAIM THAT THIS MODEL beats FABLE?
item.description: AICodeKing: AICodeKing reviews OpenRouter's Fusion API, which uses parallel model inference with a judge model to combine results. While OpenRouter claims Fable-level performance at half the price, testing across simulators, SVG generation, math, and coding tasks shows inconsistent or poor performance compared to using a single strong model. The reviewer concludes Fusion is overhyped and does not genuinely surpass Fable.

item.title: RIP Claude Fable, open-source AI unleashed, full body avatars, new Google models, new TTS: AI NEWS
item.description: AI Search: This AI news roundup covers recent developments including GLM 5.2, Kimi K2.7, MiniMax M3, and Gemini live translation. It also features discussions of new models like DiffusionGemma, video generation tools like VideoMDM and Millivid, and TTS systems like Dots. The video touches on the discontinuation of Claude Fable.

item.title: Is This The FASTEST AI Model In The World?!! (Xiaomi MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed)
item.description: Better Stack: Xiaomi and TileRT released MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed, a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model achieving over 1,000 tokens per second on standard hardware. The model uses selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel runtime to optimize inference speed.

item.title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej discusses the banning of Fable 5, framing it as marking the beginning of a permanent underclass. The video appears to connect product restrictions to broader concerns about AI access and opportunity.

item.title: This Is Bad... They Just Shut Down FABLE 5
item.description: AI Revolution: A YouTube video discussing Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models being shut down by US government order after three days of public availability. The video questions implications for future frontier AI access and whether powerful models will remain available to the public or be restricted to government control.

item.title: GPT-6 Is Launching Into a World OpenAI No Longer Controls
item.description: AI Master: A video essay analyzes leaked GPT-6 rumors and OpenAI's competitive position, arguing that Microsoft, Apple, and Google have moved away from OpenAI while GPT-5.5 shipped instead of GPT-6. The piece covers big tech partnerships, benchmark rankings, and claims about OpenAI's financial and legal challenges.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
item.description: Cole Medin: Cole Medin discusses the removal of Claude Fable 5 shortly after launch, reportedly due to US government export-control directives and claimed security vulnerabilities. He reviews Fable 5's performance metrics against Claude Opus 4.8 and reflects on implications for developers building on frontier models.

item.title: 1,000+ Tokens/Sec: Google Just Shattered the AI Speed Limit (DiffusionGemma)
item.description: Better Stack: DiffusionGemma is Google DeepMind's text generation model that applies diffusion-based image generation techniques to LLM inference. It uses uniform state diffusion with bidirectional passes over a noise canvas to achieve generation speeds exceeding 1,000 tokens per second on H100 GPUs by shifting from memory-bound to compute-bound execution.

item.title: This AI Chinese model is 15X cheaper?
item.description: Jack Roberts: MiniMax M3 is a long-context language model released on OpenRouter that uses Sparse Attention to process large documents and codebases 15.6x faster than comparable models while reducing compute costs by approximately 95 percent.

item.title: GLM-5.2 (Fully Tested): I got EARLY ACCESS & This MODEL is CRAZY!
item.description: AICodeKing: GLM-5.2 is an improved model from Z AI featuring a 1 million token context window, expected open-weight release with MIT license, and integration with coding tools. It achieves around 81.43 on coding benchmarks, approximately 6 percent below Opus 4.8, and is offered through the GLM Coding Plan at approximately $8 per month.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

item.title: BREAKING: US Government BANNED Mythos and Fable
item.description: Better Stack: The US government has issued an export control directive forcing Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers effective immediately. The announcement comes from a Better Stack YouTube video citing a regulatory enforcement action.

item.title: Google’s New AI Just Broke The AI Speed Limit: DiffusionGemma
item.description: AI Revolution: Google released DiffusionGemma, an open AI model that generates text using a different approach and achieves up to four times faster inference on dedicated GPUs. The model features a 256-token canvas and self-correction capabilities.

item.title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
item.description: Google Antigravity: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that enables developers to build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It provides multiple interfaces including a desktop app (2.0), IDE, CLI, and SDK to support flexible agentic development workflows.

item.title: AIventure: Vibe Coding Journey
item.description: Google for Developers: AIventure is an open source retro dungeon crawler game that teaches Gen AI concepts for developers. It uses Gemma 4 open weights model to power NPC vibe coding and agentic behaviors, supports multiple serving options including local inference with Transformers.js and Ollama or cloud deployment via Gemini API.

item.title: PI AGENT FULL COURSE: Master Pi Agent in 30 Minutes
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej teaches a course on Pi Agent, a tool for building real coding agents with AI. The course covers how to master Pi Agent capabilities in 30 minutes and includes practical examples of AI-assisted coding.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Just Changed Website Design Forever
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts evaluates Claude Fable 5's capabilities for website design tasks through three difficulty levels, comparing it against Opus 4.8. He discusses token efficiency trade-offs and strategic use cases for different model sizes in web design automation.

item.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
item.description: Claude: Claude FM is a music streaming service curated for deep work and creative thinking, with content made and selected by musicians. It targets developers and builders seeking focused, distraction-free environments.

item.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
item.description: Claude: Claude FM is a music streaming service curated by musicians and designed to accompany thinking and building work. The service offers background music content available on YouTube.

item.title: How to Use Higgsfield AI Like a PRO – AI Marketing Workflow
item.description: AI Master: Higgsfield Supercomputer is an AI super-agent that converts a single idea into a full marketing campaign including TV ads, podcast clips, vertical reels, and character-driven shorts. The tool uses agentic AI to maintain character and style consistency across multiple content formats and automates the traditional shoot-edit-repeat workflow.

item.title: Converting SQL Server code to PostgreSQL
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud demonstrates using Gemini as an AI tutor to convert SQL Server T-SQL code to PostgreSQL, including translation of data types like IDENTITY and BIT. The tool explains conversion logic to help developers understand PostgreSQL migration.

item.title: The Rise of Generative UI for Developers (CopilotKit)
item.description: Better Stack: Better Stack reviews CopilotKit, a framework enabling generative UI and agent-native app development. The video explains how CopilotKit addresses limitations of bolted-on AI chatbots by allowing agents to render real UI components, share state with React/Next.js apps, and implement human-in-the-loop workflows, comparing it against Vercel AI SDK and custom implementations.

item.title: How I make my AI Agent remember everything
item.description: JavaScript Mastery: JavaScript Mastery demonstrates a technique for implementing persistent memory in AI agents. The approach enables agents to retain and recall information across multiple interactions and sessions.

item.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
item.description: EO: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how a Paris-based startup with an AI performance management platform competes against enterprise giants 600x larger. She explains why companies like Google rely on spreadsheets for strategic planning, the advantages of building a full enterprise platform from day one, and her hiring philosophy focused on raising organizational standards.

item.title: Don’t miss this Claude update
item.description: Jack Roberts: Claude Opus 4.8 introduces Dynamic Workflows, which launches hundreds of subagents simultaneously within a single session. The system can split complex work into parallel tasks with built-in verification, allowing faster execution of large-scale projects like codebase analysis and multi-file research.

item.title: The $20,000 API Key Mistake
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI presents a case study of a $20,000 incident involving improper API key handling. The video serves as a warning about the financial risks of security mismanagement in AI API usage.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 & Apple’s NVIDIA deal
item.description: IBM Technology: IBM Technology's Mixture of Experts podcast hosted by Tim Hwang analyzes Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model, particularly its tier routing system, and discusses Apple's shift away from on-device AI to partner with NVIDIA for cloud compute. The episode also debates whether AI models can detect sarcasm and the role of multimodal inputs versus context alone.

item.title: The “Final Boss” of AI Workflows: UltraCode
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI discusses UltraCode as a solution for AI workflows, framing it as an advanced or comprehensive approach to workflow management. The video title suggests UltraCode represents a significant capability in the AI workflow space.

item.title: Claude Code (Fable) V/S Codex (GPT-5.5) : MATHEMATICAL TEST = ONE CLEAR WINNER!
item.description: AICodeKing: The video compares subscription-based AI services (ChatGPT Plus, ChatGPT Pro, Claude Pro, Claude Max) with API pricing models. SemiAnalysis estimates show that lower-tier subscriptions can provide hundreds to thousands of dollars in equivalent API value monthly, making them valuable for heavy users but less critical for occasional users or production workflows.

item.title: Master Claude Code with Claude Fable 5 (57 mins)
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI's 57-minute video demonstrates advanced use of Claude Code as a coding system rather than a chat interface. The video covers Desktop, CLI, and web modes, permissions, connectors, slash commands, plugins, routines, sub-agents, and dynamic workflows for building apps and automating development workflows.

item.title: Code with Claude Tokyo 2026: Opening Keynote
item.description: Claude: Anthropic is hosting the Code with Claude 2026 opening keynote in Tokyo, Japan. Engineering and product leaders from the company will present the latest updates at this event.

item.title: The Fable 5 Backlash Is Getting Serious
item.description: AI Revolution: Claude Fable 5 is facing backlash over overly restrictive guardrails that reject harmless prompts and silently fall back to weaker models without transparency. The controversy centers on whether closed AI systems with hidden behavioral constraints can be trustworthy for advanced work.

item.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

item.title: Yossi Matias on the golden age of research
item.description: Google for Developers: Yossi Matias, Vice President and Head of Google Research, discusses Google's "magic cycle" of research translating discoveries into real-world impact. The conversation covers AI applications including flood forecasting, MedGemma, Gemini for Science, and emerging areas like quantum computing with the Willow chip.

item.title: Your first agent configuration with MCP and Looker
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech tutorial on building AI-powered agents using the Agent Development Kit and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrated with Looker for enterprise data grounding. Covers MCP configuration, Looker API setup, Python environment configuration, and creating functional agents that translate prompts into data requests.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 + YouTube = $30,000/mo
item.description: Jack Roberts: A workflow for building automated YouTube channels by reverse-engineering successful creators, writing scripts with Claude, recording voice-overs, and using Higsfield CLI for automated video editing and composition. The approach aims to produce monetizable content at low cost per video.

item.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
item.description: AI Engineer: WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace that answers business questions in natural language by querying Snowflake, Linear, and Notion without manual engineer involvement. The tool uses LLMs to generate reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cost-effective.

item.title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
item.description: Cursor: Cursor's Sam Whitmore discusses with Baseten representatives the future of multi-agent systems at scale, including running hundreds of coding agents in parallel, enabling inter-agent communication and review, and building coordinated systems rather than isolated parallel tasks.

item.title: What Should An AI Device Look Like? — With Alex Himel
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, discusses how AI-powered glasses could become the next major computing platform. The conversation covers how AI assistants could enhance daily tasks, meetings, fitness, and real-world context, along with competitive pressures from OpenAI, Google, Apple, and Amazon, privacy concerns around facial recognition, and Meta's strategic pivot toward AI wearables.

item.title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)
item.description: Jordan Urbs: Jordan Urbs demonstrates how to run two types of AI agents locally: Odysseus (a workspace agent) for task orchestration and OpenCode (a build agent) for code generation. The setup uses Gemma 4 via Ollama for local inference with Venice API as fallback, deployed in Docker for privacy-focused local execution.

item.title: 21 Hermes Lessons to Get You Started
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts provides 21 lessons as an introductory guide to Hermes. The content is structured as practical guidance for users getting started with the technology.

item.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
item.description: AI Engineer: WebMCP is a proposed web standard that exposes structured tools to AI agents instead of requiring DOM scraping and pixel-coordinate clicking. It provides both declarative HTML-attribute-based APIs and imperative JavaScript APIs for registering typed, named actions that agents can call directly. The feature is in early preview on Chrome 146.

item.title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
item.description: Code With Aarohi: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard protocol that enables AI agents and applications to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a common interface. MCP uses JSON-RPC communication between clients and servers, supporting both local and remote connections via STDIO or HTTP transport mechanisms.
